steps.title: Find the singular subject
steps.content: The subject is The Proto-Nilotic language, which is singular. The phrase common ancestor of fifty-five African languages with similar linguistic properties adds description but does not change the subject's number.

steps.title: Reject plural verbs
steps.content: B, C, and D use plural verb forms, so they do not agree with the singular subject. The sentence needs a singular present-tense verb before hypothetical. The answer is A.
